item.title: Apparent hantavirus outbreak kills 3 on cruise ship, sickens at least 3 more
item.description: A suspected outbreak of the rare hantavirus on a cruise ship in the Atlantic off Africa killed three people and sickened at least three others, health officials say.

item.title: Iran attacks ship in Strait of Hormuz as U.S. begins Project Freedom
item.description: The U.S. military says two commercial ships transited the Strait of Hormuz under Project Freedom, but the UAE says one of its vessels came under attack.

item.title: 2 people die after drowning in suspected rip currents in Florida: "It's so tragic"
item.description: Local authorities in Florida say two people died over the weekend after drowning in suspected rip currents. Cristian Benavides shows how to spot a rip current and what to do if you get caught in one.

item.title: 2 missing U.S. soldiers in Morocco were hiking when they fell off cliff, officials say
item.description: Aircraft have been searching the Moroccan coastline for two U.S. soldiers who have been missing since Saturday night. According to defense officials, the service members were off-duty and hiking when they fell off a cliff and went crashing into the waves below. Officials say the soldiers were involved in an overseas training mission. Chris Livesay reports.

item.title: Suspected hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship kills 3, health officials say
item.description: Health officials say six people were sickened and three have died from a suspected hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship. Hantavirus is a rare virus spread by rodents. Ramy Inocencio reports.

item.title: Trump says U.S. will guide trapped ships through Strait of Hormuz, as gas prices surge
item.description: President Trump said on Sunday the U.S. "will guide" trapped ships from countries not involved in the war with Iran through the Strait of Hormuz while maintaining the U.S. blockade on Iranian ports. It comes as the key waterway continues to impact gas prices in the U.S. with costs up 35 cents in just the past week.

item.title: Shooting at lake near Oklahoma City leaves at least 13 injured
item.description: At least 13 people were at hospitals following a shooting at a party at Arcadia Lake, near Oklahoma City, police say.

item.title: 9 tribes sue to stop exploratory drilling in Black Hills near sacred site
item.description: Nine Native American tribes in South Dakota, North Dakota and Nebraska are suing the federal government in a bid to stop exploratory drilling for graphite near a sacred site in the Black Hills.

item.title: What does Spirit Airlines' shutdown mean for travelers?
item.description: Have tickets to fly on Spirit? Here's what to know about refunds and alternative flights now that the budget airline is ceasing operations this.

item.title: Iran hangs 3 people over involvement in anti-government protests
item.description: Arrests and executions in Iran have been on the rise since the start of the regional war triggered by a U.S.-Israeli attack on Feb. 28.

item.title: U.S. beginning effort to guide stranded ships through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump announced the U.S. would "guide" ships not involved in the war with Iran out of the Strait of Hormuz. A U.S.-led task force says it has started the operation.

item.title: Rudy Giuliani hospitalized in critical condition, spokesperson says
item.description: There was no immediate reason given for the 81-year-old's hospitalization.

item.title: Video shows United flight strike truck on N.J. Turnpike before landing at Newark
item.description: An investigation is underway after a United Airlines plane struck a light pole and truck on the New Jersey Turnpike as it was coming in for a landing at Newark Liberty Airport on Sunday afternoon, officials said.

item.title: 5/3/2026: Disaster Tourists; Birds Of War; Perfume Capital Of The World
item.description: First, when the volunteer helping after a hurricane is a white nationalist. Then, birders flock here since guerrillas disarmed. And, Grasse: The perfume capital of the world.

item.title: 5/3: CBS Weekend News
item.description: Passengers deal with Spirit shutdown fallout; Trump's poll numbers tank as Iran war continues to jack up gas prices.

item.title: 2 U.S. service members missing amid training exercise in Morocco
item.description: The service members were participating in African Lion, the largest joint military exercise on the continent.

item.title: Why some white nationalists are showing up to areas hit by natural disasters
item.description: After natural disasters, white nationalists, militias, and conspiracists often arrive, offering help. But they also want to recruit and improve their image.

item.title: Historian Jill Lepore on constitutional amendments | 60 Minutes
item.description: As the U.S. prepares to mark the 250th anniversary of American independence, historian Jill Lepore says now is a good time to ask if the public wants to see constitutional amendments.

item.title: Inside Grasse, where flowers for Chanel No. 5 have been grown for more than a century
item.description: Centuries ago, the French town of Grasse reeked of pungent odors from leather tanning. But now it's famous for quite the opposite scent from the acres of jasmine that it grows for top perfume houses.

item.title: Birding takes flight after peace deal between Colombia and guerrilla group | 60 Minutes
item.description: In Colombia, decades of fighting between the government, left-wing guerrillas, right-wing paramilitaries, and narco-traffickers preserved bird habitats in Colombia.

item.title: When the volunteer helping after a hurricane is a white nationalist | 60 Minutes
item.description: After natural disasters, white nationalists, militias, and conspiracists often arrive, offering help. But they also want to recruit and improve their image.

item.title: A tool to help keep dementia in check
item.description: Many people fear that a family history of dementia dooms them to inevitably suffer the condition themselves. But a new tool, the Brain Care Score, shows how lifestyle changes can be beneficial, cutting the risk of dementia.

item.title: U.K. police say terrorism not suspected in blast that killed 2
item.description: British police say they are not investigating an explosion that left two dead on Sunday as a terror incident, after officials raised the U.K.'s terrorism threat level.

item.title: Drugmaker asks Supreme Court to halt ruling blocking online access to mifepristone
item.description: A maker of the widely used abortion pill mifepristone​ asked the Supreme Court on Saturday to block an appellate court ruling that cut off mail-order access to the drug just a day earlier.

item.title: What states could try to redistrict and add more GOP seats
item.description: The Supreme Court's ruling on Wednesday about Louisiana's congressional map could have implications for several states, as it narrowed the section of the Voting Rights Act about majority-minority districts.

item.title: Judge rebukes prosecutors in hearing for accused D.C. gala gunman
item.description: A federal judge scolded prosecutors for pushing to move forward with detention proceedings for accused correspondents' dinner gunman Cole Allen, even though Allen agreed to remain in custody.

item.title: Artemis crew surprises 5-year-old aspiring astronaut with special gift
item.description: The Artemis II team gained a new member, and the crew made sure their youngest teammate had the right stuff for space.

item.title: Artemis II crew reveals what snack they ate right after splashdown
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts said they actually really enjoyed the space food, but it was a familiar candy they enjoyed after splashing down in the Pacific Ocean.

item.title: Artemis II astronauts talk mission toilet troubles: "We made do"
item.description: The plumbing issues aboard the Orion capsule became headline news in the early days of the historic Artemis II mission.

item.title: Artemis II astronauts join "CBS Mornings" for a special live hour Friday
item.description: A few weeks back from their trip around the moon, the Artemis crew joins CBS News for "Artemis II: A Celebration of Heroes," a town hall featuring questions from students, and special guests.
